summary: Strong Jerry banter, standout Standing On The Moon & Comes A Time
blurb: The show features an epic Standing On The Moon that many call the centerpiece, plus solid versions of West L.A. Fadeaway and Cumberland Blues. Jerry’s banter and guitar work are consistently praised, while the Drums/Space segment delivers tight rhythmic interplay. Overall the performance is above average with a few uneven moments.
review: On April 6, 1994 at Miami Arena the Dead delivered a set that felt part rehearsal, part concert, giving fans a rare glimpse into their mid‑’90s soundcheck vibe. Jerry Garcia was in top form, trading witty stage banter for his “pretty playing” and launching an unforgettable Standing On The Moon that reviewers describe as “going OFF.” The first set moved from Jack Straw through Row Jimmy with the usual energy, while West L.A. Fadeaway shone as a smooth groove. In the second set the band built momentum into a tight Drums/Space stretch before soaring through The Wheel, All Along The Watchtower and closing with that stellar moon‑lit anthem. Highlights like Comes A Time (caught in rehearsal) showcase Jerry’s playful mentoring of Vince and Bob, adding character to an otherwise solid night. Though “Easy Answers” showed up a bit too often and the overall flow is less polished than a classic tour show, the strong moments—especially the standout Standing On The Moon—make this recording worth a spin for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings (3) praise Jerry’s playing and the epic Standing On The Moon, while several note solid jams like Comes A Time and strong Drums/Space; only one negative review and a couple of mixed notes temper enthusiasm, yielding a moderate positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard capture of the rare Comes A Time soundcheck with clear instrument separation and standout Standing On The Moon performance.

key_highlights: Epic Standing On The Moon that dominates the second set
Comes A Time soundcheck jam highlighted by Jerry’s banter
Tight Drums/Space segment showcasing rhythmic interplay
West L.A. Fadeaway delivers a solid groove in the first set

band_performance.Jerry: Shows strong, pretty playing with frequent banter; vocal intensity and guitar work on Standing On The Moon are repeatedly praised, and his guidance during jams adds character.
band_performance.Phil: Provides solid bass foundation, notably highlighted on Broken Arrow where he "kicks ass as usual."
band_performance.Bob: Occasionally receives Jerry’s coaching (e.g., on Easy Answers) but does not stand out prominently in the recordings.
band_performance.Vince: Receives guidance from Jerry during the soundcheck; no major solo moments noted.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers tight, energetic drumming throughout, especially in the Drums/Space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey for a powerful rhythmic foundation, contributing to the strong Drums/Space flow.
